questions.text: Which phase of the cell cycle consists of DNA replication?
questions.explanation: DNA replication occurs during the S phase of interphase.
Mitosis creates two genetically identical daughter cells.
Homologous chromosomes exchange segments during prophase I.
Meiosis produces haploid cells from a diploid parent.

questions.text: The final physical separation of daughter cells is called:
questions.explanation: Cytokinesis divides the cytoplasm and completes cell division.

questions.text: Which phase follows G2 in the cell cycle?
questions.explanation: After G2, the cell enters M phase where mitosis occurs.
